exercises.title: Animal Hierarchy with Inheritance
exercises.description: Create an Animal base class and Dog/Cat subclasses. Each subclass should call super().__init__() and add its own specific method.
exercises.starterCode:
class Animal:
    def __init__(self, name, age):
        self.name = name
        self.age = age

    def describe(self):
        return f"{self.name} (age {self.age})"

    def eat(self, food):
        print(f"{self.name} eats {food}")


class Dog(Animal):
    def __init__(self, name, age, breed):
        # TODO: Call super().__init__(name, age)
        # TODO: Set self.breed = breed
        pass

    def bark(self):
        # TODO: Print: "Fido (Labrador) says: Woof!"
        pass

    def describe(self):
        # TODO: Return parent describe + ", Breed: Labrador"
        # Hint: base = super().describe()
        pass


class Cat(Animal):
    def __init__(self, name, age, indoor):
        # TODO: Call super().__init__(name, age)
        # TODO: Set self.indoor = indoor (True/False)
        pass

    def meow(self):
        # TODO: Print: "Whiskers says: Meow!"
        pass


# Create instances
fido = Dog("Fido", 3, "Labrador")
whiskers = Cat("Whiskers", 5, True)

fido.eat("kibble")  # Inherited
fido.bark()         # Dog-specific
print(fido.describe())

whiskers.eat("tuna")  # Inherited
whiskers.meow()        # Cat-specific
print(f"Indoor cat: {whiskers.indoor}")

print(isinstance(fido, Animal))
exercises.expectedOutput:
Fido eats kibble
Fido (Labrador) says: Woof!
Fido (age 3), Breed: Labrador
Whiskers eats tuna
Whiskers says: Meow!
Indoor cat: True
True
hints: def describe(self): base = super().describe(); return f'{base}, Breed: {self.breed}'
isinstance(fido, Animal) returns True because Dog inherits from Animal

exercises.title: Shape Hierarchy with super()
exercises.description: Build a Shape → Rectangle → Square hierarchy. Each level adds its own attributes and uses super() to build on the parent.
exercises.expectedOutput:
Rectangle (blue): area=24.00
Square (red): area=25.00
Square is a Rectangle: True
Square is a Shape: True
hints: class Rectangle(Shape): def __init__(self, w, h, color='white'): super().__init__(color); self.width = w; self.height = h
class Square(Rectangle): def __init__(self, side, color='white'): super().__init__(side, side, color)
self.__class__.__name__ gives the actual class name ('Square' for a Square instance)

exercises.title: Employee Hierarchy
exercises.description: Create an Employee base class and a Manager subclass. Manager overrides calculate_pay() to add a 10% bonus and adds a method to manage direct reports.
exercises.starterCode:
class Employee:
    company = "TechCorp"

    def __init__(self, name, employee_id, salary):
        self.name = name
        self.employee_id = employee_id
        self.salary = salary

    def calculate_pay(self):
        """Monthly pay."""
        return self.salary / 12

    def get_info(self):
        return f"{self.name} ({self.employee_id}) - ${self.salary:,.0f}/yr"


class Manager(Employee):
    def __init__(self, name, employee_id, salary, department):
        # TODO: Call super().__init__
        # TODO: Set self.department and self.reports = []
        pass

    def add_report(self, employee):
        # TODO: Append employee to self.reports
        pass

    def calculate_pay(self):
        # TODO: Get base pay from super().calculate_pay()
        # TODO: Return base * 1.10 (10% bonus)
        pass

    def get_info(self):
        # TODO: Return parent info + department + report count
        # e.g., "Alice Johnson (M001) - $96,000/yr | Engineering | 2 reports"
        pass


# Create employees
alice = Manager("Alice Johnson", "M001", 96000, "Engineering")
bob = Employee("Bob Smith", "E001", 72000)
carol = Employee("Carol Lee", "E002", 68000)

alice.add_report(bob)
alice.add_report(carol)

print(alice.get_info())
print(bob.get_info())
print(f"Alice monthly pay: ${alice.calculate_pay():,.2f}")
print(f"Bob monthly pay: ${bob.calculate_pay():,.2f}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
Alice Johnson (M001) - $96,000/yr | Engineering | 2 reports
Bob Smith (E001) - $72,000/yr
Alice monthly pay: $8,800.00
Bob monthly pay: $6,000.00
hints: calculate_pay in Manager: base = super().calculate_pay(); return base * 1.10
get_info in Manager: base = super().get_info(); return f'{base} | {self.department} | {len(self.reports)} reports'

warmup.title: Quick Recall: IS-A Relationship
warmup.description: What does this code print? Think about what isinstance returns for inherited classes.
